intro: Lachemilla pseudovenusta is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae), native to the high-altitude Andean regions of South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to soothe digestive issues and reduce stress levels. The bioactive constituents of Lachemilla pseudovenusta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
